title: Star Wars: The Jedi Academy Trilogy

'''''Star Wars: The Jedi Academy Trilogy''''' is a book trilogy written by [[Kevin J. Anderson]] and published by [[Bantam Spectra]] from [[March]] [[1994]] to [[October]] of the same year. The novels are ''[[Jedi Search]]'', [[Dark Apprentice (novel)|''Dark Apprentice'']], and ''[[Champions of the Force]]''.<ref name="TERC" />

The books tell the story of [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker's]] attempt to [[New Jedi Order|resurrect]] the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] on [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]], and his search for possible [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] candidates.

There are several stories told throughout the three books. The main plot is that of [[Exar Kun/Legends|Exar Kun's]] spirit attempting to turn Luke's trainee Jedi, he kills [[Gantoris|one of them]] and turns [[Kyp Durron]] who goes on a rampage with the [[Sun Crusher]]. The Jedi trainees eventually destroy Kun and Kyp is brought back to the light by his friend [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]].

==Plot summary==
On a [[Diplomat/Legends|diplomatic]] mission for the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|Millennium Falcon]] is shot down over [[Kessel/Legends|Kessel]], and [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] and [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]] are [[Prison/Legends|imprisoned]] within the grueling [[Spice mines of Kessel/Legends|spice mines of the forsaken world]]. [[Moruth Doole]], paranoid [[Administrator/Legends|administrator]] of the mines, is afraid the new government will take control of his operations. While imprisoned, Han and Chewie befriend [[Kyp Durron]], a young [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] who helps them escape the pitch-black tunnels and commandeer an [[Imperial shuttle (disambiguation)|Imperial shuttle]]. Fleeing Kessel, Kyp uses [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] to navigate a path to safety through the [[Maw Cluster/Legends|black holes of the Maw]] and stumbles upon the [[Maw Installation]], a secret [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] research facility under the command of [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Natasi Daala|Daala]]. Han, Chewie, and Kyp once again find themselves [[Prisoner/Legends|prisoners]]. At the Maw Installation, they befriend [[Qwi Xux]], a brilliant but naïve Imperial [[Scientist/Legends|scientist]] who was part of the team that helped engineer the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]]. Han explains to her how her scientific breakthroughs were used for massive destruction. Qwi is shocked by this, and in a conscientious effort to redeem her work she helps the new prisoners escape aboard a new experimental [[Superweapon/Legends|weapon]], the [[Sun Crusher]]. The weapon is a small [[Starship/Legends|vessel]] protected by nigh-destructible [[Quantum-crystalline armor]] and armed with [[Resonance torpedo|resonance torpedoes]] that can provoke a [[Supernova/Legends|supernova]] in a targeted [[Star/Legends|star]], thus wiping out entire [[Star system/Legends|star systems]]. Rather than let such a potent weapon fall into irresponsible hands, the New Republic sinks the Sun Crusher into the depths of the [[Gas giant/Legends|gas giant]] [[Yavin/Legends|Yavin]].

[[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] sets out to establish a [[Jedi Praxeum|Jedi academy]] to begin training a new generation of [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]]. Using partial records captured from scattered sources, Luke finds twelve Force-sensitive candidates to train, including [[Gantoris]] of [[Eol Sha]], [[Streen]] of [[Bespin/Legends|Bespin]], [[Kirana Ti]] of the [[Witches of Dathomir|Dathomirian Witches]], the [[Cloning/Legends|cloned]] [[Dorsk 81]], and Kyp Durron, who exhibits enormous Force potential. Luke sets up his training center in the abandoned temples of [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]], where the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebels]] previously hid their headquarters. The New Republic suffers a series of tragedies and setbacks. [[Mon Mothma/Legends|Mon Mothma]] falls ill with a mysterious wasting disease and asks [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia]] to serve in her stead. Admiral [[Gial Ackbar/Legends|Ackbar]], while visiting the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Vortex/Legends|Vortex]], suffers inexplicable mechanical failure in his modified [[A/SF-01 B-wing starfighter/Legends|B-wing fighter]], crashing the vessel and [[Death/Legends|killing]] hundreds of innocents. Inconsolable, Ackbar resigns his command. Admiral Daala, now aware of the state of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] outside the Maw Cluster, begins a battery of attacks against the New Republic, striking at [[Dantooine/Legends|Dantooine]] and [[Dac/Legends|Mon Calamari]]. On Yavin 4, the [[Force ghost/Legends|spirit]] of [[Exar Kun/Legends|Exar Kun]] – a long dead [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] – begins to corrupt Luke's students. First, Gantoris falls to the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] and attempts to attack Luke and then is utterly destroyed by the Sith spirit. Then Kun possess Kyp. 

Doing Kun's biding, Kyp attacks and incapacitates Luke, then steals the Sun Crusher vessel, resurrecting it from the heart of Yavin. He wipes Qwi Xux's memories to ensure that the technological knowledge required to build such a device is lost. In a strike of vengeance against Admiral Daala, Kyp uses the Sun Crusher to ignite the stars of the [[Cauldron Nebula/Legends|Cauldron Nebula]], causing a devastating explosion that Daala narrowing avoids. Kyp, and instrument of destruction, adopts the mantle of [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]]. Luke's Jedi students band together to protect his body from the dark side and do battle of the spirit of the Sith Lord. Even young [[Darth Caedus|Jacen Solo]], just three [[Standard year/Legends|years]] old, picks up a [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] – guided by Luke’s spirit – to defend his uncle. Luke is revived, but the threat of Exar Kun remains. The possessed Kyp next targets the Carida system in his swath of vengeance-fueled of destruction. He comes to the horrific realization that his brother, [[Zeth Durron|Zeth]], is stationed on Carida. Kyp fails to rescue him before the world is eradicated by the star's explosion. Mon Mothma's illness is a [[Nanovirus|nanovirus]] engineered by [[Ambassador/Legends|Ambassador]] [[Furgan]] of [[Carida/Legends|Carida]], who has been using such deceptive measures to unsettle the New Republic. His [[Agent/Legends|agent]], [[Terpfen]], was subjected to mind control and [[Saboteur/Legends|sabotaged]] Ackbar's fighter. Luke's student [[Cilghal]] uses her [[Force healing/Legends|Force healing]] abilities to remove the virus from Mothma's body, but Furgan continues his strikes, this time attacking the planet [[Anoth/Legends|Anoth]], where the youngest [[Solo family/Legends|Solo child]], [[Anakin Solo|Anakin]], resides. Ackbar and [[Winter Celchu|Winter]] defend the [[Youngling/Legends|child]], and Terpfen, shaking off his programming, kills Furgan.

Han Solo is able to reach out to Kyp and get him to surrender. New Republic officials call for Kyp's execution for punishment of his crimes, but he instead takes up arms against Admiral Daala's renewed campaign. She has returned to the Maw and unleashed another experimental weapon against the New Republic – a [[Death Star prototype|prototype Death Star]]. In a final battle over Kessel, adjacent to the Maw Cluster, Kyp uses the Sun Crusher to destroy the Imperial weapon, then drops the Sun Crusher into a black hole before escaping within a messenger capsule. The Kessel operation is in shambles after Daala's attack, but [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]] sees an opportunity to rebuild it. The spirit of Exar Kun is exorcised from Kyp, and he heals from the ordeal of Sith possession. Daala escapes aboard the [[Imperial I-class Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] ''[[Gorgon]]'' and flees into space. Though Mon Mothma is regaining her health, she nonetheless relinquishes her title of [[Chief of State (New Republic)/Legends|Chief of State]], bestowing it upon Leia as her successor.

==Development==
In the original outline for the ''Jedi Academy Trilogy'' had [[Mon Mothma/Legends|Mon Mothma]] died as a result of her infection. [[Lucasfilm]] rejected this idea, keeping Mothma alive in books through the end of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] era. <ref name="TERC" />

In November of [[1994]], to promote the upcoming release of the [[Science Fiction Book Club]] edition of the ''Jedi Academy'' trilogy in an omnibus hardcover, the series publisher, [[Bantam Spectra]], released a TV commercial for the series.

==Continuity==
The later novel ''[[I, Jedi]]'' was a [[Retroactive continuity|retcon]] of much of the action at the [[Jedi academy]], inserting [[Corran Horn/Legends|Corran Horn]] into the storyline and smoothing out parts that did not conform to later continuity.
